1.name: note
1.description: The scribbling on the note has been worn away and can no longer be read.

2.name: map
2.description: This map was once readable, but no longer. Maybe you can get a new one?

3.name: crypt key
3.description: An ancient and crude key. I wonder where it could be used?

4.name: winterfire crystal
4.description: This crystal is extraordinarily rare and capable of generating a localized, gentle warmth when activated.

5.name: amethyst
5.description: The amethyst crystal is a beautiful hue of purple.

6.name: sleeping bag
6.description: Use this to get some quick sleep

7.name: residence key
7.description: A key to a residence in Frostfang. It's a bit rusty, but it should still work.

8.name: lockpick kit
8.description: A set of lockpicks. You can use these to unlock doors and chests.

9.name: pinecone grenade
9.description: Throw this to cause a stir!

10.name: history of frostfang
10.description: The book tells of a time Frostfang was known as "DragonsFang", and was not in a permanent winter.

11.name: rat trap
11.description: A simple wooden box with a hinged lid, containing a spring-loaded mechanism designed to snap shut on small creatures.

12.name: eldertree blossom
12.description: A small, white flower that grows on ancient elder trees, revered for its healing and protective qualities.

13.name: celestial lotus
13.description: A rare aquatic flower with petals that shimmer like stars, believed to enhance one's connection to the celestial realms.

14.name: moonshadow orchid
14.description: A delicate, luminescent flower that glows softly in the moonlight, often used in lunar magic rituals.

15.name: inferno rose
15.description: A striking deep orange and red rose with petals that resemble flames, often used in rituals invoking passion and transformation.

16.name: aurora heartbloom
16.description: A multicolored flower that changes hues with the dawn and dusk, symbolizing love and new beginnings.

17.name: shadowfern
17.description: A dark green fern with shadowy fronds, used in concealment spells and for creating safe havens.

18.name: mystic seed packets
18.description: Assorted packets of rare and enchanted seeds, each claimed to have unique magical properties.

19.name: the shadow herbarium
19.description: A collection of the most dangerous plants used in shadow magic and dark rituals, with instructions for safe handling.

20.name: junk
20.description: A broken object that can no longer be recognized.

21.name: stat coupon
21.description: Grants a stat point when used.

22.name: training coupon
22.description: Grants a training point when used.

23.name: rope
23.description: A basic rope. Useful when you need it, a burden when you don't.

24.name: spellbound projectiles
24.description: A book that describes spells used for casting magical projectiles.

25.name: stormwatcher key
25.description: A large key made of iron.

26.name: broom
26.description: A straw broom. You can <ansi fg="command">sweep broom</ansi> to use it.

27.name: crocodile leather
27.description: The tough hide of a crocodile.

100.name: newbie kit
100.description: A package containing helpful items to get started.

101.name: arcane flute
101.description: A flute with faintly glowing markings all along it. Maybe you can <ansi fg="command">play</ansi> it.

102.name: room rental
102.description: Rent a room in the Inn and rest up.

10002.name: guardsman's broadsword
10002.description: The standard issue weapon of a guardsman.

10003.name: captain's broadsword
10003.description: This broadsword has special decorations commemorating a captains promotion.

10004.name: dagger
10004.description: A plain dagger that still has a bit of a stabby end left.

10005.name: obsidian dagger
10005.description: The dagger, forged from pure obsidian, possesses an enigmatic allure with its glossy, jet-black blade that glistens ominously in the light.

10006.name: glowing battleaxe
10006.description: The battleaxe, robust and formidable, commands attention with its radiant glow that emanates from the core of its blade.

10007.name: finely crafted shortsword
10007.description: The finely crafted shortsword boasts a perfectly balanced blade, its gleaming steel edge honed to a razor-sharp finish, while the ornate hilt fits snugly in the hand, promising precision and grace in every strike.

10008.name: wooden claw
10008.description: The Wooden Claw is a unique hand-to-hand combat weapon, meticulously carved from durable hardwood.

10009.name: ancient royal scepter
10009.description: It's old and crude, but was once the symbol of a king.

10010.name: cudgel
10010.description: The cudgel is a robust, unassuming weapon, fashioned from sturdy oak hardened by time and use. It has a heft to it that feels solid in the grip, reassuring the wielder of its reliability in combat. The wood bears the scars of countless clashes, its surface marred with nicks and stains that speak to its history of service.

10011.name: spider fang
10011.description: The fang of a large spider.

10012.name: crowbar
10012.description: A long and thin bar of iron. Useful for prying.

10014.name: sling
10014.description: A couple of leather straps and a pouch to hold a rock. What more could you want?

10015.name: crude cudgel
10015.description: A crudely fashioned cudgel, with large nails haphazardly hammered into it, penetrating and sticking out through at various spots.

10016.name: boat oar
10016.description: An oar used to paddle a small boat.

10017.name: dancing needle
10017.description: A tiny bladed weapon the size of a sewing needle.

10018.name: jeweled dagger
10018.description: The jeweled dagger, with its gleaming blade and hilt encrusted with shimmering gems, exudes an aura of both lethal elegance and mystical power.

10019.name: pruning shears
10019.description: Pruning shears that glow softly in the moonlight, perfect for nighttime gardening.

10020.name: long whip
10020.description: A long whip used by circus performers.

20001.name: rat pelt
20001.description: The skin of a dead rat. Maybe you can get a few coins for it.

20002.name: cape
20002.description: A plain cape that protects (poorly) from the elements.

20006.name: tattered pants
20006.description: A pair of poorly held together pants.

20007.name: rusty pot
20007.description: It just barely works as a helmet.

20009.name: cloth belt
20009.description: Just enough to hold your pants up.

20010.name: giant rat pelt
20010.description: The skin of a dead giant rat. It's just big enough to wear over your head.

20011.name: giant rat tail
20011.description: Just big enough to use as a belt.

20012.name: shadowsteel breastplate
20012.description: The Shadowsteel breastplate is a marvel of craftsmanship and arcane engineering, meticulously forged from a rare and enigmatic alloy known only to a select few master smiths.

20013.name: rotting belt
20013.description: The belt, once an item of great craftsmanship, is now decayed and rotting.

20014.name: rotting boots
20014.description: The boots of a king. A dead one, anyways.

20015.name: royal ring
20015.description: Though ancient, the ring is in terrific shape.

20016.name: torn gloves
20016.description: The gloves are in terrible shape.

20017.name: chain coif
20017.description: The chain coif is a little rusty, but still in good shape.

20018.name: circlet
20018.description: The circlet of an ancient king.

20020.name: leather cap
20020.description: A leather cap that provides minimal protection.

20021.name: tattered cape
20021.description: A cape eaten by moths and rats.

20022.name: initiates robe
20022.description: The Initiate's Robe is a stark, utilitarian garment, symbolizing the acolyte's commitment to their somber duties. It is made of durable, dark fabric that hangs heavy on the wearer, designed to withstand the dampness and decay of the catacombs. The robe is unadorned save for a few simple, geometric patterns stitched along the hem and cuffs, signifying the wearer's rank within the order. The hood is practical in shape, meant to shield the eyes from the glare of torches and to preserve the wearer's anonymity. The cut is loose, allowing for ease of movement during rituals and ceremonies. Its simplicity is its statement, reflecting the solemn vows of its wearer to eschew worldly vanity and embrace the shadowed path they tread.

20023.name: spider exoskeleton
20023.description: The empty husk of a spider.

20025.name: sophie's locket
20025.description: A locket with a drawing inside.

20026.name: spiked collar
20026.description: A leather collar with metal spikes.

20027.name: gnarled wood ring
20027.description: A hand crafted ring from an unidenfiable wood.

20028.name: padded shoes
20028.description: Hand cobbled shoes made for traversing a forest.

20029.name: leather pants
20029.description: A pair of hand stitched leather pants.

20031.name: spider queen breastplate
20031.description: The Spider Queen Breastplate offers exceptional protection against both physical and magical threats. Its surface is resistant to blades and projectiles, while the enchantments woven into the chitin provide a safeguard against arcane assaults. The armor fits snugly, yet comfortably, against the wearer's torso, enhancing their natural agility rather than impeding it.

20032.name: swimming flippers
20032.description: Specially crated shoes that allow you to swim in deep water.

20034.name: leather robe
20034.description: The leather robe, commonly worn by Mystarion citizens, is a versatile and elegant garment crafted from supple, enchanted leather that offers both protection and style. The robe's exterior is a deep, rich brown, embossed with intricate, arcane patterns that subtly glow in low light, hinting at its magical properties. The interior is lined with soft, comfortable fabric, making it suitable for both everyday wear and long hours of study or travel. A hood, often draped casually over the shoulders, provides an added air of mystery and protection against the elements. Fastened with ornate clasps and adorned with small, practical pockets, the leather robe is a testament to the seamless blend of practicality and enchantment in Mystarion's fashion.

20037.name: priests ring
20037.description: A hand crafted ring with a priestly insignia.

20038.name: ice armor
20038.description: Armor chiseled from solid ice, it is cold to the touch and shimmers with a faint blue light.

20039.name: students amulet
20039.description: A simple iron amulet signifying that you are in training.

20040.name: floofy gloves
20040.description: The skin of a dead snow floof. Floofskin is known to keep you warm in extreme weather.

20041.name: snow wolf mane
20041.description: The Snow Wolf Mane is a rare and highly prized item found only on the alpha wolves of the Whispering Wastes. This thick ruff of fur runs from the top of the wolf's head down along its powerful neck and shoulders, and is distinct for its silver-white strands that shimmer like frost in the moonlight. Coarser than the rest of the wolf's coat, the mane serves as both armor against the biting cold and a display of dominance, bristling when the wolf is threatened or preparing to strike. Legends among the nomads of the Wastes claim that wearing a Snow Wolf Mane grants the bearer enhanced endurance and resistance to cold, a reflection of the wolf's mastery over the frozen wilderness.

20042.name: wolf pelt
20042.description: The shiny pelt of a timber wolf.

20043.name: graduation cap
20043.description: A special hat signifying that you've completed the tutorial.

20044.name: teaching cap
20044.description: A special hat signifying that you're worthy to teach a subject.

20045.name: rat-skin cape
20045.description: A patchwork cape made up of many rat hides.

30001.name: small red potion
30001.description: A small red potion... you COULD drink it...

30002.name: ambrosia potion
30002.description: Rumored to be from the gods themselves.

30003.name: abyssal nectar
30003.description: Imbibable essence of the abyss itself.

30006.name: mug of ale
30006.description: Some ale to wash down your meal.

30008.name: goldenbell
30008.description: A bright yellow herb that chimes when touched, used to summon light and enhance joy in rituals.

30009.name: glacial mint
30009.description: A frosty herb that cools the air around it, perfect for ice spells and calming potions.

30010.name: moonshade leaf
30010.description: A silvery leaf that glows faintly in the dark, used to enhance dream magic and nocturnal rituals.

30011.name: dreamweaver's tea
30011.description: A soothing lavender and chamomile blend that promotes restful sleep and vivid dreams.

30012.name: phoenix elixir
30012.description: A fiery potion made from sunfire blooms that revitalizes and boosts energy levels.

30013.name: midnight bliss
30013.description: A dark, velvety potion crafted from glimmering nightshade and shadowfern, known for its deep, rich flavor and mysterious allure, perfect for those seeking an enigmatic and profound experience.

30014.name: small blue potion
30014.description: A small blue potion... you COULD drink it...

30015.name: waterskin
30015.description: A waterskin that holds several glugs.

30016.name: spotted mushroom
30016.description: A small red capped mushroom with white spots. Small magenta spores occasionally fall from it.

30017.name: polymorph potion
30017.description: A scintillating rainbow potion, teeming with bubbling activity.
